publications.title: Structural Basis for Clonal Diversity of the Public T Cell Response to a Dominant Human Cytomegalovirus Epitope.
publications.authors: Yang X, Gao M, Chen G, Pierce BG, Lu J, Weng NP, Mariuzza RA.
publications.executive_summary: Two structurally distinct public TCRs (C7 and C25) specific for the HLA-A2–restricted immunodominant CMV epitope NLVPMVATV were characterized by crystal structure determination in complex with peptide-MHC. Despite recognizing the same peptide-MHC ligand, C7 and C25 employ completely different CDR3α and CDR3β motifs and contact the NLV peptide through divergent footprints, with C25 focusing on the central peptide region while C7 engages more broadly. The structures demonstrate how the same public CDR3 motifs can pair with different variable regions and how different variable regions can associate with the same CDR3 motif, providing multiple structural solutions for binding identical pMHC. This modularity of TCR variable and CDR3 elements explains how a dominant public T cell response to a persistent pathogen—characterized by clonal diversity and frequently shared TCRs across unrelated individuals—can emerge despite structural heterogeneity. The findings illuminate the molecular basis for the reproducible yet multisolution nature of human immune responses to viral pathogens.
